mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.16% is near the zero lower bound, constraining further monetary easing. As inflation (CPIAUCSL at 231.025) remains elevated relative to the Fed's 2% target, expectations of future rate hikes will push the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 20 trading days as markets price in eventual normalization.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20-day forward window in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 7.8% signals slack labor market. Sustained high unemployment suppresses wage growth expectations and inflation expectations, leading the Fed to maintain accommodative policy longer than markets initially priced. This extends the duration of low real yields, pushing nominal 10-year Treasury yields downward as growth expectations remain muted.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of instances when UNRATE > 7.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST at 861k annualized) and retail sales (RSAFS at 415.7B) indicate recovering demand. Strong demand growth signals future inflation and economic expansion, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to rise as investors demand higher compensation for inflation and duration risk over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 58% of triggered instances, or correlation between demand composite and 20d yield change drops below 0.15 over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO at 97.509) remains below pre-crisis trend. Weak supply-side capacity utilization constrains real output growth, reducing inflation pressure and keeping real yields low. The 10-year Treasury yield will decline as markets recognize structural supply constraints limit nominal growth potential over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production index at 97.5, below 100 trend
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 62% of 20-day forward windows, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 24-month period.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS at 134.021M) is growing but remains below pre-crisis peak employment. Weak labor market momentum signals subdued aggregate demand and inflation expectations. The Fed will maintain accommodative policy, anchoring long-term yields downward as growth expectations remain constrained over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 65% of 60-day forward windows when PAYEMS < 135000, or Rank IC < 0.06 over rolling 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1 at 13652.5B) growth has slowed. Weak demand growth combined with elevated unemployment creates a deflationary bias. Markets will price in extended monetary accommodation, causing the 10-year Treasury yield to decline as real yields compress and nominal growth expectations fall over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:Weak growth with slack labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield rises in > 60% of 5-day forward windows when both conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.08 over rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL at 231.025) is elevated relative to the Fed's implicit 2% target. As inflation expectations become unanchored, the Fed will signal future rate increases to restore credibility. Market expectations of higher future short rates will push the 10-year Treasury yield upward over the next 60 trading days as the term premium expands.
regime_hint: inflation persistence with policy normalization expectations
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 62% of 60-day forward windows when CPIAUCSL > 230, or Rank IC < 0.05 over rolling 24-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS at 415.695B) and housing starts (HOUST at 861k) both show recovery momentum. Strong demand growth will accelerate inflation expectations and economic growth forecasts. The 10-year Treasury yield will rise as investors demand higher yields to compensate for expected inflation and growth over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield falls in > 65% of 5-day forward windows when all three conditions are met, or Rank IC < 0.07 over rolling 12-month windows.
